name: Principles of Physics
description: Mechanics. Introduction to general principles and analytical methods used in physics with emphasis on applications in applied agricultural and biological sciences and in physical education.
rawPrerequisitesText: Trigonometry or consent of instructor.

name: Principles of Physics
description: Continuation of PHY 001A. Heat, optics, electricity, modern physics.
rawPrerequisitesText: PHY 001A or PHY 009A.

name: General Physics
description: Introduction to general principles and analytical methods used in physics for students majoring in a biological science.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 016B (can be concurrent) or MAT 017B (can be concurrent) or MAT 021B (can be concurrent); completion or concurrent enrollment in MAT 017B or MAT 021B.

name: General Physics
description: Continuation of PHY 007A.
rawPrerequisitesText: PHY 007A C- or better.

name: General Physics
description: Continuation of PHY 007B.
rawPrerequisitesText: PHY 007B C- or better.

name: Classical Physics
description: Introduction to general principles and analytical methods used in physics for physical science and engineering majors. Classical mechanics.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 021B C- or better or MAT 021BH C- or better or MAT 021M C- or better or MAT 017C C- or better or MAT 017B B or better.

name: Classical Physics
description: Continuation of PHY 009A. Fluid mechanics, thermodynamics, wave phenomena, optics.
rawPrerequisitesText: (PHY 009A C- or better or PHY 009HA C- or better); (MAT 017C C- or better or MAT 021C C- or better or MAT 021CH C- or better).

name: Classical Physics
description: Electricity and magnetism including circuits and Maxwell's equations.
rawPrerequisitesText: PHY 009A C- or better or PHY 009HA C- or better; PHY 009B C- or better; MAT 021D C- or better.

name: Modern Physics
description: Introduction to physics concepts developed since 1900. Special relativity, quantum mechanics, atoms, molecules, condensed matter, nuclear and particle physics.
rawPrerequisitesText: PHY 009B C- or better; PHY 009C C- or better; (MAT 022A C- or better or MAT 027A C- or better or MAT 067 C- or better); (MAT 022B (can be concurrent) or MAT 027B (can be concurrent))

name: Honors Physics
description: Classical mechanics. Same material as PHY 009A in greater depth. For students in physical sciences, mathematics, and engineering.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 021B (can be concurrent); or MAT 021BH (can be concurrent) or MAT 021M (can be concurrent); or consent of instructor.

name: Honors Physics
description: Special relativity, thermal physics. Continuation of PHY 009HA.
rawPrerequisitesText: PHY 009HA C- or better or PHY 009A B- or better; MAT 021C (can be concurrent) or MAT 021CH (can be concurrent).

name: Honors Physics
description: Waves, sound, optics, quantum physics. Continuation of Physics 009HB.
rawPrerequisitesText: PHY 009HA C- or better or PHY 009A C- or better; PHY 009HB C- or better; MAT 021C C- or better or MAT 021CH C- or better.

name: Honors Physics
description: Electricity and magnetism. Continuation of Physics 009HC.
rawPrerequisitesText: PHY 009HB C- or better; PHY 009HC C- or better; MAT 021D C- or better.

name: Honors Physics
description: Application of quantum mechanics.
rawPrerequisitesText: PHY 009HD; (MAT 022B or MAT 027B (can be concurrent)).

name: Topics in Physics for Nonscientists
description: Emphasis varies: survey of basic principles or a deeper exploration of some particular branch. Past topics included black holes, space time, and relativity; physics of music; history and philosophy; energy and the environment; and natural phenomena.
rawPrerequisitesText: High school algebra.

name: Physics of California
description: Atmospheric phenomena common in CA, local weather patterns and microclimes. Applications to CA energy, water, and resource management policies. Physics underlying regional sports in CA.

name: Physics of California
description: Conceptual understanding of the physics underlying regional sports in CA. Focus on skiing, surfing, and scuba diving. Atmospheric phenomena common in CA, local weather patterns and microclimes, applications to CA energy, and water are also discussed.

name: Visualization in Science
description: Production, interpretation, and use of images in physics, astronomy, biology, and chemistry as scientific evidence and for communication of research results.

name: Fractals, Chaos & Complexity
description: This version has ended; see updated course, below. Modern ideas about the unifying ideas of fractal geometry, chaos and complexity. Basic theory and applications with examples from physics, earth sciences, mathematics, population dynamics, ecology, history, economics, biology, computer science, art and architecture.
rawPrerequisitesText: MAT 016A or MAT 017A or MAT 019A or MAT 021A.

name: Introduction to Computational Physics
description: Introduction to programming with examples from numerical analysis and computational physics. Introduction to modern tools used for scientific analysis and computer algebra.

name: Computational Physics
description: Algorithms and programming techniques of computational physics with applications from introductory physics.
rawPrerequisitesText: PHY 040; PHY 009C or PHY 009HD.

name: Experimental Techniques
description: Experimental techniques. Design of circuits. Data analysis, sources of noise,  statistical and systematic uncertainties. Light sources, detection, and measurement in basic optical systems.
rawPrerequisitesText: PHY 009C C- or better or PHY 009HD C- or better.

name: Lower Division Seminar
description: Examination of a special topic in Physics through shared readings, discussions, written assignments, or special activities such as laboratory work.
rawPrerequisitesText: Consent of instructor; lower division standing.

name: Directed Group Study
description: Directed group study.
rawPrerequisitesText: Consent of instructor. Primary for lower division students.

name: Special Study for Undergraduates
description: Special study for undergraduates.
rawPrerequisitesText: Consent of instructor.

name: Computational Laboratory in Physics
description: Introduction to computational physics and to the computational resources in the physics department. Preparation for brief programming assignments required in other upper division physics classes.
rawPrerequisitesText: (MAT 021D,  (MAT 022A or MAT 027A),  (MAT 022B or MAT 027B)),  (PHY 009D or PHY 009HD),  PHY 040,  PHY 104A (can be concurrent).

name: Introduction to Mathematical Methods in Physics
description: Introduction to mathematics used in upper division physics courses, including applications of vector spaces, Fourier analysis, and partial differential equations.
rawPrerequisitesText: (PHY 009C C- or better or PHY 009HD C- or better); MAT 021D C- or better; (MAT 022A C- or better or MAT 027A C- or better or MAT 067 C- or better); (MAT 022B C- or better or MAT 027B C- or better); PHY 040 (can be concurrent).

name: Computational Methods of Mathematical Physics
description: Introduction to the use of computational techniques to solve the mathematical problems that arise in advanced physics courses, complementing the analytical approaches emphasized in PHY 104A.
rawPrerequisitesText: PHY 104A C- or better; PHY 105AL; or consent of instructor.

name: Intermediate Methods of Mathematical Physics
description: Applications of complex analysis, conditional probability, integral transformations and other advanced topics.
rawPrerequisitesText: PHY 104A C- or better; or consent of instructor.

name: Classical Mechanics
description: Principles and applications of Newtonian mechanics. Introduction to Lagrangian and Hamiltonian mechanics.
rawPrerequisitesText: (PHY 009A C- or better or PHY 009HA C- or better); (PHY 009C C- or better or PHY 009HD C- or better); (MAT 022A C- or better or MAT 027A C- or better or MAT 067 C- or better); (MAT 022B (can be concurrent) or MAT 027B (can be concurrent)); or consent of instructor.

name: Analytical Mechanics
description: Principles and applications of Newtonian mechanics; introduction to Lagrange’s and Hamilton’s equations.
rawPrerequisitesText: PHY 104A C- or better; PHY 105A C- or better; or consent of instructor.

name: Continuum Mechanics
description: The continuum hypothesis and limitations, tensors, isotropic constitutive equations, and wave propagation. Applications such as elastic solids, heat flow, aerodynamics, and ocean waves.
rawPrerequisitesText: PHY 104A C- or better; PHY 105A C- or better; or consent of instructor.

name: Optics
description: The phenomena of diffraction, interference, and polarization of light, with applications to current problems in astrophysics, material science, and atmospheric science. Study of modern optical instrumentation.
rawPrerequisitesText: (PHY 009C B+ or better or PHY 009HD B+ or better or (PHY 110A C- or better, PHY 110B { can be concurrent })), (MAT 022A B+ or better or MAT 027A B+ or better or MAT 067 B+ or better or PHY 104A C- or better); or consent of instructor.

name: Optics Laboratory
description: Laboratory consists of one major project pursued throughout the quarter, based on modern applications of optical techniques.
rawPrerequisitesText: PHY 108 C- or better (can be concurrent); or consent of instructor.

name: Electricity & Magnetism
description: Theory of electrostatics, electromagnetism, Maxwell's equations, electromagnetic waves.
rawPrerequisitesText: (PHY 009C C- or better or PHY 009HD C- or better); PHY 104A C- or better; or consent of instructor.

name: Electricity & Magnetism
description: Theory of electrodynamics and radiation.
rawPrerequisitesText: (PHY 009D C- or better or PHY 009HB C- or better); PHY 104A C- or better; PHY 110A C- or better; or consent of department.

name: Electricity & Magnetism
description: Theory of electrostatics, electromagnetism, Maxwell's equations, electromagnetic waves.
rawPrerequisitesText: PHY 110B C- or better; or consent of department.

name: Computational Physics Lab for Electricity & Magnetism
description: Applications of computational physics to problems from electricity and magnetism.
rawPrerequisitesText: PHY 040 C- or better; (PHY 045 C- or better or ECS 032C C- or better or ECS 036B C- or better); PHY 110A C- or better; PHY 110B (can be concurrent); or consent of instructor.

name: Thermodynamics & Statistical Mechanics
description: Introduction to classical and quantum statistical mechanics and their connections with thermodynamics. Theory is developed for the ideal gas model and simple magnetic models and then extended to studies of solids, quantum fluids, and chemical equilibria.
rawPrerequisitesText: (PHY 009B C- or better or PHY 009HB C- or better); (PHY 009D C- or better or PHY 009HC C- or better); PHY 104A C- or better; PHY 115A (can be concurrent); or consent of instructor.

name: Computational Physics Lab for Statistical Mechanics
description: Applications of computational physics to problems from statistical mechanics.
rawPrerequisitesText: PHY 040 C- or better; (PHY 045 C- or better or ECS 032C C- or better or ECS 036B C- or better); (PHY 112 (can be concurrent) or CHE 110C (can be concurrent)); or consent of instructor.

name: Foundation of Quantum Mechanics
description: Introduction to the methods of quantum mechanics with applications to atomic, molecular, solid state, nuclear and elementary particle physics.
rawPrerequisitesText: ((PHY 009C C- or better, PHY 009D C- or better) or (PHY 009HC C- or better, PHY 009HD C- or better)); PHY 104A C- or better; PHY 105A C- or better; or consent of department.

name: Applications of Quantum Mechanics
description: Angular momentum and spin; hydrogen atom and atomic spectra; perturbation theory; scattering theory.
rawPrerequisitesText: PHY 104A C- or better; PHY 115A C- or better; or consent of instructor.

name: Computational Physics Lab for Quantum Mechanics
description: Applications of computational physics to problems from Classical and Quantum Mechanics.
rawPrerequisitesText: PHY 040 C- or better; (PHY 045 C- or better or ECS 032C C- or better or ECS 036B C- or better); (PHY 115A C- or better or CHE 110A C- or better); or consent of instructor. PHY 115B recommended concurrently.

name: Electronic Instrumentation
description: Experimental and theoretical study of important analog electronic circuits. Linear circuits, transmission lines, input impedance, feedback, amplifiers, oscillators, noise.
rawPrerequisitesText: (PHY 009C or PHY 009HD); (MAT 022B or MAT 027B); or consent of instructor.

name: Electronic Instrumentation
description: Continuation of PHY 116A. Introduction to the use of digital electronics and microcomputers in experimental physics. Nonlinear electronics, integrated circuits, analog-to-digital and digital-to-analog converters, transducers, actuators.
rawPrerequisitesText: PHY 009C or PHY 009HD; or consent of instructor.

name: Introduction to Computer-Based Experiments in Physics
description: Introduction to techniques for making physical measurements using computer-based instrumentation.
rawPrerequisitesText: (PHY 009D or PHY 009HD); PHY 116B; MAT 022B; or consent of instructor.

name: Physics Instrumentation with Analog & Digital Electronics
description: Experimental and theoretical study of important electronic circuits involving analog and digital components. Feedback, amplifiers, oscillators, noise, integrated circuits, digital logic, timers, analog-to-digital and digital-to-analog converters.
rawPrerequisitesText: PHY 080.

name: Physics Instrumentation for Data Acquisition
description: Experimental application of modern high-density integrated circuits. Automated data acquisition, microprocessors, field programmable gate arrays.
rawPrerequisitesText: PHY 080; PHY 045 or ECS 036B.

name: Advanced Laboratory in Condensed Matter Physics
description: Experimental techniques and measurements in solid-state physics. Three-six experiments performed depending on difficulty. Individual work is stressed. Thorough write-ups of the experiments are required.
rawPrerequisitesText: PHY 080 C- or better; PHY 110A C- or better; PHY 115A C- or better; PHY 110B C- or better (can be concurrent); PHY 112 C- or better (can be concurrent); PHY 115B recommended (can be concurrent).

name: Advanced Laboratory in Particle Physics
description: Experimental techniques and measurements in nuclear and particle physics. Perform three to six experiments depending on difficulty. Individual work is stressed. Thorough write-ups of the experiments are required.
rawPrerequisitesText: PHY 080 C- or better; PHY 110A C- or better; PHY 115A C- or better; PHY 110B C- or better (can be concurrent); PHY 112 C- or better (can be concurrent); PHY 115B recommended (can be concurrent).

name: Signals & Noise in Physics
description: Techniques of measurement and analysis designed to avoid systematic error and maximize signal/noise ratio. Illustrative examples of optimal filters ranging from condensed matter to cosmology.
rawPrerequisitesText: (PHY 009A,  PHY 009B,  PHY 009C,  PHY 009D) or (PHY 009HA,  PHY 009HB,  PHY 009HC,  PHY 009HD,  PHY 009HE); PHY 104A; or consent of instructor.

name: Introduction to Nuclear Physics
description: Survey of basic nuclear properties and concepts requiring introductory knowledge of quantum mechanics: nuclear models and forces, radioactive decay and detecting nuclear radiation and nuclear reaction products, alpha, beta and gamma decay.
rawPrerequisitesText: PHY 115A C- or better; or consent of instructor.

name: Elementary Particle Physics
description: Properties and classification of elementary particles and their interactions. Experimental techniques. Conservation laws and symmetries. Strong, electromagnetic, and weak interactions. Introduction to Feynman calculus.
rawPrerequisitesText: PHY 104A C- or better; PHY 115A C- or better; PHY 115B (can be concurrent);or consent of instructor.

name: Elementary Particle Physics
description: Properties and classification of elementary particles and their interactions. Experimental techniques. Conservation laws and symmetries. Strong, electromagnetic, and weak interactions. Introduction to Feynman calculus.
rawPrerequisitesText: PHY 104A C- or better; PHY 115A C- or better; PHY 115B C- or better; or consent of instructor; PHY 130A recommended.

name: Introduction to Solid State Physics
description: Survey of fundamental ideas in the physics of solids, with selected device applications. Crystal structure, x-ray and neutron diffraction, phonons, simple metals, energy bands and Fermi surfaces, semiconductors, optical properties, magnetism, superconductivity.
rawPrerequisitesText: (PHY 009D B or better or PHY 009HC B or better or PHY 115A C- or better); PHY 104A C- or better; or consent of instructor.

name: Introduction to Solid State Physics
description: Survey of fundamental ideas in the physics of solids, with selected device applications. Crystal structure, x-ray and neutron diffraction, phonons, simple metals, energy bands and Fermi surfaces, semiconductors, optical properties, magnetism, superconductivity.
rawPrerequisitesText: (PHY 009C A- or better or PHY 009HD A- or better or (PHY 110A C- or better, PHY 110B (can be concurrent))), PHY 112 C- or better, PHY 115A C- or better, PHY 140A C- or better; PHY 115B recommended.

name: Special Topics in Physics
description: Topics vary, covering areas of contemporary research in physics.
rawPrerequisitesText: (PHY 009A,  PHY 009B,  PHY 009C,  PHY 009D) or (PHY 009HA,  PHY 009HB,  PHY 009HC,  PHY 009HD,  PHY 009HE); or consent of instructor.

name: Stellar Structure & Evolution
description: Chemical composition, structure, energy sources and evolutionary history of stars, with equal emphasis on both the observational data and theoretical models, including black holes, neutron stars and white dwarfs and the formation of substellar masses.
rawPrerequisitesText: (PHY 009A,  PHY 009B,  PHY 009C,  PHY 009D) or (PHY 009HA,  PHY 009HB,  PHY 009HC,  PHY 009HD,  PHY 009HE); or consent of instructor.

name: Galactic Structure & the Interstellar Medium
description: Structure, contents, and formation of our Milky Way galaxy, viz. its shape and size, the nature of the interstellar medium, stellar populations, rotation curves, mass determination and evidence of dark matter.
rawPrerequisitesText: (PHY 009A C- or better or PHY 009HA C- or better); (PHY 009B C- or better or PHY 009HC C- or better); (PHY 009C C- or better or PHY 009HD C- or better); PHY 040 (can be concurrent); PHY 105A (can be concurrent); MAT 022B (can be concurrent); or consent of instructor.

name: Extragalactic Astrophysics
description: Structure and evolution of galaxies and clusters of galaxies, including distance and mass determination, galaxy types and environments, active galactic nuclei and quasars, gravitational lensing and dark matter, global cosmological properties.
rawPrerequisitesText: ((PHY 009A,  PHY 009B,  PHY 009C,  PHY 009D) or (PHY 009HA,  PHY 009HB,  PHY 009HC,  PHY 009HD,  PHY 009HE)); PHY 104A; PHY 105A; or consent of instructor.

name: Astrophysical Applications of Physics
description: Applications of classical and quantum mechanics, thermodynamics, statistical mechanics, and electricity and magnetism to astrophysical settings such as the Big Bang, degenerate white dwarf and neutron stars, and solar neutrinos.
rawPrerequisitesText: PHY 105A; PHY 105B; PHY 110B (can be concurrent); PHY 115A (can be concurrent); PHY 112; PHY 112 or consent of instructor; PHY 110B and PHY 115A required concurrently.

name: General Relativity
description: Definition of the mathematical frame work for the description of the gravitational field, introduction of the dynamical equations of Einstein governing its evolution and review of the key solutions, including black holes and expanding universes.
rawPrerequisitesText: PHY 104A; PHY 105A; PHY 105B; PHY 110A; PHY 105B and PHY 110A or consent of instructor.

name: Introduction to Cosmology
description: Contemporary knowledge regarding the origin of the universe, including the Big Bang and nucleosynthesis, microwave background radiation, formation of cosmic structure, cosmic inflation, cosmic acceleration and dark energy.
rawPrerequisitesText: ((PHY 009A, PHY 009B, PHY 009C, PHY 009D) or (PHY 009HA, PHY 009HB, PHY 009HC, PHY 009HD, PHY 009HE)); PHY 105A (can be concurrent); or consent of instructor.

name: Astronomy Instrumentation & Data Analysis Laboratory
description: Experimental techniques, data acquisition and analysis involving laboratory astrophysics plus stellar, nebular and galaxy digital imaging, photometry and/or spectroscopy. Students perform three experiments. Individual work stressed. Minimum 10-15 page journal style articles of two experiments are required.
rawPrerequisitesText: PHY 080; PHY 104A; PHY 105A; PHY 110A; PHY 110B (can be concurrent); PHY 115A (can be concurrent); and consent of department.

name: Formation of Galaxies & Cosmic Structures
description: Formation of the cosmic web, including dark-matter halos and galaxies within them. Components of the Universe (dark matter, gas, stars) and their dynamical evolution. Gravitational collapse and virialization. Gas dynamics, heating, and cooling. Galactic star formation and stellar dynamics. Emphasis on theoretical concepts, computational methods, and simulations.
rawPrerequisitesText: PHY 040 C- or better; (PHY 009D C- or better or PHY 009HB C- or better); PHY 104A C- or better; PHY 105A C- or better; or consent of instructor.

name: Alumni Seminar Series
description: Weekly guest speakers (usually a physics alumnus or alumna) tell students about their careers. Speakers use their experience to give students valuable perspectives on life after a degree in physics.

name: Careers in Physics
description: Overview of important research areas in physics, discussions of research opportunities and internships, strategies for graduate school and industrial careers, the fellowship and assistantship selection process, preparation of resumes, personal statements, and letters of recommendation.

name: Internship in Physics
description: Supervised work experience requiring the application of physics principles and techniques in a professional setting, including but not limited to industry and national laboratories.
rawPrerequisitesText: Consent of instructor/Physics Internship Director.

name: Special Study for Honors Students
description: Open only to Physics and Applied Physics majors who satisfy the College of Letters and Science requirements for entrance into the Honors Program. Independent research project at a level significantly beyond that defined by the normal physics curriculum.
rawPrerequisitesText: Consent of instructor.

name: Special Study for Honors Students
description: Open only to Physics and Applied Physics majors who satisfy the College of Letters and Science requirements for entrance into the Honors Program. Independent research project at a level significantly beyond that defined by the normal physics curriculum.
rawPrerequisitesText: Consent of instructor.

name: Senior Thesis
description: Open only to Physics and Applied Physics majors with senior standing. Preparation of a senior thesis on a topic selected by the student with approval of the department.
rawPrerequisitesText: Consent of instructor.

name: Tutoring in Physics & Astronomy
description: Tutoring of students in lower division courses. Leading of small voluntary discussion groups affiliated with one of the department's regular courses. Weekly meeting with instructor.

name: Directed Group Study
description: Directed group study.
rawPrerequisitesText: Consent of instructor.

name: Special Study for Advanced Undergraduates
description: Special study for advanced undergraduates.

name: Theory of Mechanics & Electromagnetics
description: Theoretical approaches in classical mechanics including the use of generalized coordinates and virtual work; variational calculus; Lagrange equations; symmetries, conservation laws, and Noether theorem; Lagrangian density; Hamilton formalism; canonical transformations; Poisson brackets; and Hamilton-Jacobi equations.
rawPrerequisitesText: PHY 204A (can be concurrent); PHY 104B; PHY 105B; PHY 110C; or equivalent to PHY 110C; PHY 204A required concurrently.

name: Theory of Mechanics & Electromagnetics
description: Theoretical approaches in electromagnetics including static electromagnetic fields; Maxwell's equations; plane waves in various media; magnetohydrodynamics; diffraction theory; radiating systems; and special relativity.
rawPrerequisitesText: PHY 104A B or better; PHY 110B B or better; or graduate standing in Physics and Astronomy.

name: Theory of Mechanics & Electromagnetics
description: Theoretical approaches in electromagnetics including static electromagnetic fields; Maxwell's equations; plane waves in various media; magnetohydrodynamics; diffraction theory; radiating systems; and special relativity.
rawPrerequisitesText: PHY 200B B- or better; or consent of instructor.

name: Methods of Mathematical Physics
description: Linear vector spaces, operators and their spectral analysis, complete sets of functions, complex variables, functional analysis, Greens functions, calculus of variations, introduction to numerical analysis.
rawPrerequisitesText: PHY 104A; or the equivalent.

name: Methods of Mathematical Physics
description: Linear vector spaces, operators and their spectral analysis, complete sets of functions, complex variables, functional analysis, Green's functions, calculus of variations, introduction to numerical analysis.
rawPrerequisitesText: PHY 104A; PHY 104B; or the equivalent.

name: Computational Physics
description: Analytic techniques to solve differential equations and eignevalue problems. Physics content of course will be self-contained, and adjusted according to background of students.
rawPrerequisitesText: Knowledge of Fortran or C.

name: Quantum Mechanics
description: Formal development and interpretation of non-relativistic quantum mechanics; its application to atomic, nuclear, molecular, and solid-state problems; brief introduction to relativistic quantum mechanics and the Dirac equation.
rawPrerequisitesText: PHY 115B; or the equivalent.

name: Quantum Mechanics
description: Formal development and interpretation of non-relativistic quantum mechanics; its application to atomic, nuclear, molecular, and solid-state problems; brief introduction to relativistic quantum mechanics and the Dirac equation.
rawPrerequisitesText: PHY 115B; or the equivalent.

name: Quantum Mechanics
description: Formal development and interpretation of non-relativistic quantum mechanics; its application to atomic, nuclear, molecular, and solid-state problems; brief introduction to relativistic quantum mechanics and the Dirac equation.
rawPrerequisitesText: PHY 115B; or the equivalent.

name: Statistical Mechanics
description: Foundations of thermodynamics and classical and quantum statistical mechanics with simple applications to properties of solids, real gases, nuclear matter, etc. and a brief introduction to phase transitions.
rawPrerequisitesText: PHY 215B; or equivalent.

name: Statistical Mechanics
description: Further applications of thermodynamics and classical and quantum statistical mechanics. The modern theory of fluctuations about the equilibrium state, phase transitions and critical phenomena.
rawPrerequisitesText: PHY 219A.

name: Group Theoretical Methods of Physics-Condensed Matter
description: Theory of groups and their representations with applications in condensed matter.
rawPrerequisitesText: PHY 215A; PHY 215B; PHY 215C (can be concurrent); or consent of instructor. PHY 215C required concurrently.

name: Group Theoretical Methods of Physics-Elementary Particles
description: Theory of groups and their representations with applications in elementary particle physics.
rawPrerequisitesText: PHY 215A; PHY 215B; PHY 215C (can be concurrent); or consent of instructor. PHY 215C required concurrently.

name: Nuclear Physics
description: Comprehensive study of the nucleon-nucleon interaction including the deuteron, nucleon-nucleon scattering, polarization, determination of real parameters of Smatrix, and related topics.
rawPrerequisitesText: PHY 215B.

name: Nuclear Physics
description: Study of nuclear models, including shell model, collective model, unified model. Energy level spectra, static momenta, and electromagnetic transition rates.
rawPrerequisitesText: PHY 224A.

name: Nuclear Physics
description: Study of nuclear scattering and reactions including the optical model and direct interactions. Beta decay and an introduction to weak interactions.
rawPrerequisitesText: PHY 224B.

name: Advanced Nuclear Theory
description: Advanced topics in nuclear theory; theory of quantum-mechanical scattering processes. Exact formal theory and models for two-body scattering.
rawPrerequisitesText: PHY 224C.

name: Advanced Nuclear Theory
description: Advanced topics in nuclear theory; theory of quantum-mechanical scattering processes. Exact formal theory and models for three-body scattering.
rawPrerequisitesText: PHY 229A.

name: Quantum Theory of Fields
description: Relativistic quantum mechanics of particles; techniques and applications of second quantization; Feynman diagrams; renormalization.
rawPrerequisitesText: PHY 215C.

name: Quantum Theory of Fields
description: Continuation of 230A, with selected advanced topics, such as S-matrix theory, dispersion relations, axiomatic formulations.
rawPrerequisitesText: PHY 230A.

name: Quantum Theory of Fields
description: Renormalization theory and applications, including dimensional regularization, Ward identities, renormalization group equations, coupling constant unification, and precision electroweak calculations.
rawPrerequisitesText: PHY 230A; PHY 230B; or consent of instructor.

name: Topics in String Theory
description: Current research trends in string theory, with topics ranging from perturbative worldsheet methods, nonperturbative aspects and dualities, AdS/CFT correspondence, string field theory, etc.
rawPrerequisitesText: Graduate standing in Physics or consent of instructor; PHY 230A, PHY 230B, PHY 230C  PHY 260 or equivalent are strongly recommended.

name: Advanced Topics in Geometry & Physics
description: Modern geometric methods in theoretical physics, with topics ranging from pseudo-Riemannian differential geometry and topology with application to general relativity, black holes, and string theory.
rawPrerequisitesText: PHY 230A, PHY 230B, PHY 230C,  PHY 260 or equivalent strongly recommended.

name: Condensed Matter Physics A
description: Topics in condensed matter physics: Crystal structure; one-electron theory; transport and optical properties of semiconductors; phonons, electron-phonon scattering.
rawPrerequisitesText: PHY 140A; PHY 215B; PHY 219A.

name: Condensed Matter Physics B
description: Topics in condensed matter physics: transport and optical properties of metals and quantum structures; experimental measurement the Fermi surface and of phonon spectra.
rawPrerequisitesText: PHY 240A.

name: Condensed Matter Physics
description: Review of second quantization. Interacting electron gas, electron-phonon interaction and effects, including instabilities of electronic systems. Topics in the theory of superconductivity and magnetism.
rawPrerequisitesText: PHY 240A; PHY 240B.

name: Advanced Topics in Magnetism
description: Topics chosen from areas of current research interest.
rawPrerequisitesText: PHY 240A; PHY 240B; PHY 240C; PHY 240D; or consent of instructor.

name: Advanced Topics in Superconductivity
description: Topics chosen from areas of current research interest.
rawPrerequisitesText: PHY 240A; PHY 240B; PHY 240C; PHY 240D; or consent of instructor.

name: Surface Physics of Materials
description: Experimental and theoretical fundamentals of surface and interface physics and chemistry, including electronic and magnetic structure, thermodynamics, adsorption kinetics, epitaxial growth, and a discussion of various spectroscopic and structural probes based on photons, electrons, ions, and scanning probes.
rawPrerequisitesText: PHY 140A; PHY 140B; PHY 115A; PHY 115B; or the equivalent to any; PHY 215A, PHY 240A, or the equivalents recommended.

name: Surface Physics of Materials
description: Experimental and theoretical fundamentals of surface and interface physics and chemistry, including electronic and magnetic structure, thermodynamics, adsorption kinetics, epitaxial growth, and a discussion of various spectroscopic and structural probes based on photons, electrons, ions, and scanning probes.
rawPrerequisitesText: PHY 140A; PHY 140B; PHY 115A; PHY 115B; or the equivalent to any; PHY 215A, PHY 240A, or the equivalents recommended.

name: High-Energy Physics
description: Phenomenology and systematics of strong, electromagnetic, and weak interactions of hadrons and leptons; determination of quantum numbers; quarks and quarkonia; deep inelastic scattering; the quark parton model; experiments at hadron colliders and electron-positron colliders.
rawPrerequisitesText: PHY 230A.

name: High-Energy Physics
description: Electroweak interactions; phenomenology of the Standard Model of SU(2)LxU(1); weak interaction experiments; properties of and experiments with W and Z vector bosons; Glashow-Weinberg-Salam model and the Higgs boson; introduction to supersymmetry and other speculations.
rawPrerequisitesText: PHY 245A.

name: Collider Physics
description: Collider physics. Topics include quark and gluon distribution functions and the computation of cross sections; Large Hadron Collider and International Linear Collider phenomenology; collider and detector characteristics; extracting models from data; software tools for analyzing experimental data.
rawPrerequisitesText: PHY 245A; PHY 252B (can be concurrent); or consent of instructor; PHY 252B taken previously or concurrently.

name: Supersymmetry: Theory & Phenomenology
description: Construction of supersymmetric models of particle physics; superfields; supersymmetry breaking the minimal supersymmetric standard model; supergravity. Collider phenomenology of supersymmetry. Dark matter phenomenology.
rawPrerequisitesText: PHY 230A, PHY 230B, PHY 245A, PHY 245B recommended, or consent of instructor.

name: Supersymmetry: Theory & Phenomenology
description: Construction of supersymmetric models of particle physics; superfields; supersymmetry breaking the minimal supersymmetric standard model; supergravity. Collider phenomenology of supersymmetry. Dark matter phenomenology. Not offered every year.
rawPrerequisitesText: PHY 230A; PHY 230B; PHY 245A, PHY 245B recommended or consent of instructor.

name: Advanced Supersymmetry
description: Advanced topics in supersymmetry. Topics include holomorphy, the Affleck-Dine-Seiberg superpotential, Seiberg duality for SUSY QCD, dynamical SUSY breaking, Seiberg-Witten theory, superconformal field theories, supergravity, anomaly and gaugino mediation, and the AdS/CFT correspondence.
rawPrerequisitesText: PHY 246A.

name: Special Topics in Physics
description: Topic varies.
rawPrerequisitesText: Consent of instructor.

name: Techniques of Experimental Physics
description: Introduction to techniques and methods of designing and executing experiments. Problems and examples from condensed matter research will be utilized.

name: Techniques of Experimental Physics
description: Introduction to techniques and methods of designing and executing experiments. Problems and examples from nuclear and particle research will be utilized.

name: Statistics & Data Analysis for Particle Physics
description: Introduction to statistical data analysis methods in particle physics. Theoretical lectures combined with practical computer laboratory work.

name: Signals & Noise in Physics
description: Techniques for extracting signals from noise, systematic error.

name: Econophysics
description: Application of ideas from statistical mechanics to the financial markets. Market dynamics from a physics and systems perspective, including the statistical distributions of returns, the dynamics of prices, and models for the markets.
rawPrerequisitesText: Knowledge of Python, R, Excel, MATLAB, or consent of instructor.

name: Physics of Information
description: Nonlinear dynamics, deterministic chaos, bifurcations, pattern formation, symbolic dynamics, measurement theory, stochastic processes, elementary information theory, information in complex systems, computational laboratory.
rawPrerequisitesText: Consent of instructor; advanced undergraduate or introductory graduate differential equations, applied linear algebra, and probability theory; e.g., MAT 119A/MAT 119B or MAT 207A, MAT 167 or MAT 226A, and MAT 135A/MAT 135B or MAT 235A, respectively; or in PHY 104A/PHY 104B/PHY 104C or PHY 204A/PHY 204B.

name: Physics of Computation
description: Structural complexity, computational mechanics, information measures, causal inference, applications to complex materials, quantum dynamics, and nonequilibrium thermodynamics, computational laboratory.
rawPrerequisitesText: PHY 256A; consent of instructor; advanced undergraduate or introductory graduate differential equations, applied linear algebra, and probability theory; e.g., MAT 119A/MAT 119B or MAT 207A, MAT 167 or MAT 226A, and MAT 135A/MAT 135B or MAT 235A, respectively; or in PHY 104A/PHY 104B/PHY 104C or PHY 204A/PHY 204B.

name: Introduction to General Relativity
description: An introduction to general relativity. Differential geometry and curved spacetime; the Einstein field equations; gravitational fields of stars and black holes; weak fields and gravitational radiation; experimental tests; Big Bang cosmology.
rawPrerequisitesText: PHY 200A; PHY 200B.

name: Foundations of Astrophysics & Cosmology
description: Foundational observations and models of astrophysics and cosmology. Photometry; spectroscopy; distance, velocity, and mass measurements; orbits, dark matter, and black holes; Friedmann-Lemaitre-Robertson-Walker cosmology.
rawPrerequisitesText: PHY 105A B- or better; PHY 110A B- or better; PHY 115A B- or better.

name: Early Universe Cosmology
description: Introduction to early universe cosmology: the Big Bang, inflation, primordial nucleosynthesis, dark matter, dark energy, and other topics of current interest.
rawPrerequisitesText: Second year standing in Physics Graduate Program or consent of instructor.

name: Cosmic Structure Formation
description: Growth of structure from small density inhomogeneities in the early universe to the diverse structures observable today. Use of observable properties (cosmic microwave background, gravitational lensing, peculiar velocities, number density, etc.) to constrain models of structure formation and fundamental physics.
rawPrerequisitesText: PHY 260.

name: High Energy Astrophysics & Radiative Processes
description: Survey covering galactic and extragalactic X-ray and gamma-ray astronomy, radiative processes, and techniques of high-energy astrophysics.
rawPrerequisitesText: Graduate standing in Physics or consent of instructor.

name: Data Analysis for Astrophysics
description: Survey covering measurement and signal analysis techniques for astrophysics and cosmology throughout the electromagnetic spectrum.
rawPrerequisitesText: Graduate standing in Physics or consent of instructor.

name: Observational Extragalactic Astronomy & Cosmology
description: Survey covering current areas of research on extragalactic objects, their physical properties, origin, evolution, and distribution in space.
rawPrerequisitesText: Graduate standing in Physics or consent of instructor.

name: Research Methods in Astrophysics
description: Introduction to research methods in astrophysics and cosmology. Problems and examples from observational and theoretical work will be included.
rawPrerequisitesText: PHY 204A, PHY 204B and PHY 215A recommended.

name: Stellar Structure & Evolution
description: Underlying physics of stars as the building blocks of the Universe. Reasons for their existence, mechanisms behind their function, their evolution and ultimate fate. Structure of self-gravitating objects, energy transport in stars, nuclear fusion in stars, stellar evolution, and the birth of compact objects.
rawPrerequisitesText: PHY 200A, PHY 200B, and PHY 215A recommended; graduate standing in Physics; or consent of instructor.

name: Current Topics in Physics Research
description: Reading and discussion to help physics graduate students develop and maintain familiarity with the current and past literature in their immediate field of research and related areas.
rawPrerequisitesText: Graduate standing in Physics or consent of instructor.

name: Careers in Physics
description: Designed to give Physics graduate students an in-depth appreciation of career opportunities with a graduate degree in physics. Professional physicists, mainly from outside academia, will give seminars describing  both research and career insights.
rawPrerequisitesText: Graduate standing in Physics.

name: Seminar in Physics
description: Presentation and discussion of topics of current research interest in physics. Topics will vary weekly and will cover a broad spectrum of the active fields of physics research at a level accessible to all physics graduate students.
rawPrerequisitesText: Graduate standing in Physics or consent of instructor.

name: Seminar in Nuclear Physics
description: Presentation and discussion of topics of current research interest in nuclear physics.
rawPrerequisitesText: Graduate standing in Physics or consent of instructor.

name: Seminar in Elementary Particle Physics
description: Presentation and discussion of topics of current research interest in elementary particle physics.
rawPrerequisitesText: Graduate standing in Physics or consent of instructor.

name: High Energy Frontier Initiative & Cosmology Theory Seminar
description: Weekly seminar on current issues in Particle physics and Cosmology.
rawPrerequisitesText: Physics graduate students.

name: Seminar in Condensed Matter Physics
description: Presentation and discussion of topics of current research interest in condensed matter physics.
rawPrerequisitesText: Graduate standing in Physics or consent of instructor.

name: Seminar in Cosmology
description: Presentation and discussion of topics of current research interest in Cosmology.
rawPrerequisitesText: Graduate standing in Physics or consent of instructor.

name: Introduction to Departmental Research
description: Seminar to introduce first- and second-year physics graduate students to the fields of specialty and research of the Physics staff.

name: Field, Strings, & Gravity Seminar
description: Presentation and discussion of topics of current research interest in the areas of quantum field theory, string theory and gravity.
rawPrerequisitesText: Consent of instructor.

name: Research on the Teaching & Learning of Physics
description: Discussion and analysis of recent research in how students construct understanding of physics and other science concepts and the implications of this research for instruction.
rawPrerequisitesText: Graduate standing in Physics or consent of instructor.

name: Group Study
description: Group study.
rawPrerequisitesText: Consent of instructor.

name: Research
description: Research.

name: Teaching in an Active-Engagement Physics Discussion/Lab Setting
description: Analysis of recent research on science/physics teaching and learning and its implications for teaching labs, discussions, and discussion/labs with an emphasis on differences between conventional and active-engagement instructional settings. The appropriate role of the instructor in specific instructional settings.
rawPrerequisitesText: PHY 009D; or equivalent.

name: Methods of Teaching Physics
description: Practical experience in methods and problems related to teaching physics laboratories at the university level, including discussion of teaching techniques, analysis of quizzes and laboratory reports and related topics. Required of all Physics Teaching Assistants.
rawPrerequisitesText: Consent of instructor; graduate standing in Physics.

name: Teaching Assistant Training Practicum
description: Teaching assistant training.
rawPrerequisitesText: Graduate standing.
